items.description: A deep-sea diver photographed on the ocean floor, surrounded by sediment and marine life.
Two divers in full gear stand on the deck of a ship, accompanied by crew members. The scene documents an operational or preparatory moment before or after a dive.
An underwater diver holds the air hose connected to the surface. The seabed is visible beneath him while bubbles and suspended particles fill the water.
Close-up of a diver's helmet with hoses and fittings visible. The operator's gloved hands interact with the equipment, highlighting technical details.
A diver seated on the edge of a boat is assisted by a worker. The scene shows support and equipment checks before or after a dive.
A diver climbs a metal ladder to exit the water and return to the ship's deck, holding part of his equipment while the sea stretches in the background.
A diver moves underwater with a tank on his shoulders and a safety line attached. He advances close to the seabed.
A diver explores a large submerged structure, likely part of a wreck or machinery. The object is covered in marine encrustations, indicating long submersion.
A group of divers in suits and helmets sit on the ship's deck in a row, showing uniformity in equipment and suggesting an operational unit ready for action.
A diver in a heavy suit explores the seabed using an underwater torch. The beam of light cuts through murky water as air bubbles rise toward the surface.
A diver emerges from the water beside a ship and grabs a metal ladder to climb aboard, holding part of his equipment.
A diver seated on the ship's edge is assisted by an operator who checks or adjusts equipment, with the sea in the background.
Top-down view of a diver on the deck handling a piece of equipment, surrounded by hoses and metal structures.
A diver walks slowly on the seabed near rock or coral formations, with air bubbles rising from the helmet.
A diver moves along the seabed among rock formations, with a cable connected to the surface trailing his movements.
A diver on the ship's deck holds an air hose connected to equipment during a pre-dive operational phase.
Three divers in suits and helmets stand on the ship's deck.
A diver kneels on deck inspecting or preparing equipment next to a metal device, likely part of the diving system.
A diver examines an artifact on the seabed, surrounded by sediment and marine debris.
A diver moves along the seabed while manipulating a pipe or instrument, engaged in a technical underwater task.
A deep-sea diver inspecting a creature on the seabed.
A diver moves along the seabed with a cable attached to the surface, while air bubbles rise from the helmet.
A diver walks on the seabed near rock formations, with air bubbles rising from the helmet.
A narrow underwater canyon with dark rocky walls. The sandy seabed is dotted with small corals and boulders, while diffuse light filters from above creating strong contrast.
Silhouette of a large marine mammal swimming above a coral seabed, lit by soft light from above.
Close-up of dense, rounded coral formations with detailed texture and pronounced depth.
A sandy seabed with suspended sediments creating a turbid atmosphere, with few elements visible beyond the texture.
Close-up view of the body of a large marine creature.
A flat expanse of low corals extending toward the horizon.
A school of fish silhouetted swimming toward the surface, crossing shafts of light.
A very dark underwater environment.
Silhouette of a marine creature moving through a narrow rocky passage illuminated from above.
Sandy seabed with scattered small corals and a diffuse circular light illuminating the scene.
Coral landscape with heavy suspended particles and grain, creating an almost snowy effect.
Silhouette of a marine creature swimming above a coral reef, with diffuse light in the background.
Prominent coral formation with branching details, set in semi-darkness.
Side view of a coral reef with a sloping seabed and reduced visibility.
Seabed with corals in the foreground and a blurred marine creature in the background, immersed in mist.
Coral formation with intricate details, lit by diffuse light that highlights its textures.
Sandy seabed with small corals and a diffuse circular light illuminating the scene.
Coral landscape with heavy suspended particles and grain, creating an almost snowy effect.
Silhouette of a marine creature swimming above a coral reef, with diffuse light in the background.
Prominent coral formation with branching details, set in semi-darkness.
Side view of a coral reef with a sloping seabed and reduced visibility.
Seabed with corals in the foreground and a blurred marine creature in the background, immersed in mist.
Official scientific research documents representing nautical points with varying depths on a hand written graph.
Sonar frequencies graph representing the data collected during the mission in the Aleutian Trench.
A scientific paper containing a sonar frequencies graph representing the data collected during the mission in the Aleutian Trench.
A composite archival page containing multiple technical figures, circular scans, contour diagrams, plotted data, and typed analysis discussing acoustic readings and mapped basin anomalies.
A report page with typed analysis, redacted text, waveform plots, contour illustrations, and notes describing a low-frequency acoustic tone detected during a submersible mission.
Heavily redacted official report detailing the history of the mission.
A composite archival page containing multiple technical figures, marine charts and oceanographic maps.
A single report page featuring a sonar image of a vertical formation and typed notes describing inconsistent returns, signal degradation, and total sonar attenuation.
Sonar graph showing a highly irregular waveform with annotations about frequency shifts, amplitude spikes, and a noted correlation to depth changes during the incident.
A restricted Oceanographic Research Authority addendum stating that the anomalies are of unknown origin.
A formal incident report for file D-17/6B documenting loss of contact with Nerites II on 18 June 1957, listing last transmission depth, observations, and an undetermined cause of failure.
A post-incident review page showing a grainy image of a damaged submersible-like object and typed analysis describing abrupt structural deformation without typical implosion debris patterns.
A technical chart page with colored bathymetric bands, depth curves, and typed notes about conflicting pressure gauge, cable payout, and acoustic beacon readings.
A mission authorization page for file D-17/6B.
Official acoustic data analysis showing anomalous underwater readings.

A report page with a grainy deep-sea image and typed notes describing an unidentified object at approximately 9,700 meters, persistent sonar returns, unexplained movement, and a recommendation to avoid close-range observation.
A scientific paper with graphs and handwritten notes representing different nautical points with varying depths.
A reproduced technical reference page showing descent and approach profiles for steep-wall hadal operations, with diagrams comparing expected and observed vehicle paths near submarine escarpments.

A large archival-style research collage combining landscape photographs, technical illustrations, charts, handwritten notes, and a large plotted data graph all dating back to studies prior to the missions.
Official scientific documents stating irregularities of unknown origin and not attributable to mechanical failures.
Page from an operations manual explaining how to operate a submersible and detailing the code of conduct regarding underwater operations.
An official document from the Oceanographic Research Authority noting irregularities and anomalies possibly due to issues in the equipment and requesting further investigation.
A scientific paper with graphs and handwritten notes representing the scientific findings made prior to the expedition in the Strait of Florida and signed off by the research team.
A scientific paper with graphs and handwritten graphs and nautical charts written prior to the expedition in the Strait of Florida detailing the speed of the vessel.
A scientific paper with diagrams and graphs showing the relationship between different variables found during underwater surveys.

An official document from the Oceanographic Research Authority requesting the immediate suspension of all underwater operations in the Aleutian Trench after the discovery of unusual acoustic signatures following the events of the expedition in sector D-17.
A topographic map showing underwater terrain of the Aleutian Trench and surrounding areas with detailed contour lines.

A composite archival page containing multiple technical figures, circular scans, contour diagrams, plotted data, and typed analysis discussing acoustic readings and mapped basin anomalies relative to studies on the Monterey submarine Canyon.
The front page of an official document describing submersible operation procedures and how to operate a submersible.
A scientific paper with sonar frequency data recorded during underwater surveys in the Strait of Florida underscoring the significance of the anomalies in the findings.
A scientific paper with a circular nautical map relative to the Strait of Florida.
A scientific paper containing multiple sonar frequency graphs recorded during underwater surveys in the Strait of Florida.
A scientific paper containing multiple sonar frequency graphs recorded underwater in the Strait of Florida over the course of several days.
A scientific paper containing multiple sonar frequency graphs recorded underwater in the Aleutian Trench.
A topographic map showing underwater terrain of the Strait of Florida.
